label: Eq. 1
verbatim: arg min_{W_hat} || W X - W_hat X ||_F
paraphrase: Layer-wise post-training compression objective: minimize Frobenius norm between original and compressed layer outputs given calibration input activations X.
preconditions: Per-layer optimization with limited calibration data; W in R^{m x n}, W_hat in R^{m x n}, X is the input activation of layer l.
consequences: Defines the task-specific compression loss that EoRA must indirectly minimize via low-rank residual paths.

label: Eq. 2
verbatim: arg min_{A,B} || Delta - A B^T ||_F where Delta = W - W_hat
paraphrase: Naive SVD error-approximation loss: directly approximate the compression error Delta with a rank-r product A B^T using SVD.
preconditions: SVD applied to Delta; ignores calibration X; closed-form via top-r singular values.
consequences: ZeroQuant-V2 baseline; does not minimize Eq.1 because it neglects activation distribution.

label: Eq. 3
verbatim: arg min_{A',B'} || Delta' - A' B'^T ||_F where Delta' = Delta Q' and Q' = U sqrt(Lambda)
paraphrase: EoRA loss: project compression error into the eigenspace via Q' = U sqrt(Lambda) before applying SVD; SVD(Delta') = U' Sigma' V'^T, then A' = U' Sigma', B' = V'^T.
preconditions: Eigendecomposition X_tilde X_tilde^T = U Lambda U^T where X_tilde is mean of input activations over calibration set.
consequences: Aligns approximation error with task-specific compression loss; canonical EoRA optimization (Stage 4 in spec).

label: Eq. 4
verbatim: X W_hat^T + X B^T A^T  (forward pass with EoRA where B = B' Q'^{-1})
paraphrase: Per-layer forward pass of compressed model compensated with EoRA: original compressed weight plus low-rank residual product B A.
preconditions: B and A consolidated back to original space via Q'^{-1} = sqrt(Lambda)^{-1} U^T; same dimensions as standard low-rank factors.
consequences: No additional inference latency from the eigenspace projection itself; only A,B are stored.

label: Eq. 5
verbatim: arg min_{A,B} || W X - (W_hat + A B^T) X ||_F = arg min_{A,B} || Delta X - A B^T X ||_F
paraphrase: Reformulation of the compensation objective: combined error of compressed weight plus low-rank residual on calibration activations.
preconditions: Add residual A B^T to W_hat without modifying compressed weights.
consequences: Bridges Eq.1 to Eq.6 via Gram-matrix identity.

label: Eq. 6
verbatim: arg min_{A,B} || Delta X - A B^T X ||_F = arg min_{A,B} [ trace( (Delta - A B^T) X X^T (Delta - A B^T)^T ) ]^{1/2}
paraphrase: Frobenius norm rewritten via Gram matrix X X^T trace identity.
preconditions: Frobenius norm equals square root of trace of Gram matrix.
consequences: Naive SVD on Delta does not minimize this; motivates eigenspace projection.

label: Theorem 1
verbatim: For an activation matrix X, whose matrix product X X^T has an eigendecomposition given by X X^T = U Lambda U^T. By projecting the compression error Delta into the eigenspace with U sqrt(Lambda) as Delta' = Delta U sqrt(Lambda), minimizing Eq. 3 via SVD becomes equivalent to minimizing Eq. 6.
paraphrase: Eigenspace projection makes SVD on the projected error optimal for the calibration-weighted layer loss.
preconditions: X X^T symmetric (always holds); eigendecomposition exists.
consequences: Theoretical guarantee that EoRA minimizes layer-wise compression loss without gradients; key justification for Stage 4.

label: Eq. 7
verbatim: [ trace( (Delta - A B^T) U Lambda U^T (Delta - A B^T)^T ) ]^{1/2} = [ trace( (Delta U - A B^T U) Lambda (Delta U - A B^T U)^T ) ]^{1/2}
paraphrase: Substituting X X^T = U Lambda U^T into Eq. 6.
preconditions: Trace cyclic and orthogonality of U.
consequences: Step in proof of Theorem 1.

label: Eq. 8
verbatim: [ trace( (Delta U sqrt(Lambda) - A B^T U sqrt(Lambda)) (Delta U sqrt(Lambda) - A B^T U sqrt(Lambda))^T ) ]^{1/2}
paraphrase: Splitting Lambda = sqrt(Lambda) sqrt(Lambda) and absorbing into both sides of the residual.
consequences: Reveals projected error Delta' = Delta U sqrt(Lambda).

label: Eq. 9
verbatim: [ trace( (Delta' - B'^T) (Delta' - B'^T)^T ) ]^{1/2} = || Delta' - A' B'^T ||_F  (with A' = U' Sigma', B'^T = V'^T after SVD)
paraphrase: Square root of Gram matrix recovered as Frobenius norm; Eckart-Young gives optimal rank-r solution via SVD on Delta'.
consequences: Closes the proof: SVD on Delta' is optimal for Eq.6.

label: Algorithm 1 (EoRA)
verbatim: Input: X_tilde (mean of input activations over calibration set), W (full-precision), W_hat (compressed), r (rank). Output: A,B. Steps: (1) Delta = W - W_hat; (2) eigendecompose X_tilde X_tilde^T = U Lambda U^T; (3) reformulate U Lambda U^T = (U sqrt(Lambda)) (sqrt(Lambda) U^T) = Q' Q'^T; (4) project Delta' = Delta Q'; (5) r-rank SVD on Delta', U' Sigma' V'^T = SVD(Delta'); (6) project back B = V'^T Q'^{-1}; (7) forward pass: X W_hat^T + X B^T A^T.
paraphrase: Seven-step training-free EoRA pipeline producing low-rank residual factors A and B per linear layer.
preconditions: Average activation X_tilde computed over calibration set; eigendecomposition stable.
consequences: Canonical reference implementation; Stage 4 in spec follows steps 1-6 verbatim. Q'^{-1} computed as sqrt(Lambda)^{-1} U^T.

label: training-free
verbatim: EoRA—a novel, fine-tuning-free method that augments compressed LLMs with low-rank matrices ... avoids backpropagation and completes in just a few minutes using minimal calibration data.
paraphrase: EoRA is fully training-free: no gradients, no backprop, only eigendecomposition + SVD on calibration activations.
preconditions: Compressed model already produced (e.g., via SparseGPT or GPTQ); calibration set available.
consequences: Spec must NOT introduce gradient-based training in Stage 4; any optimizer step would violate paper claim.

label: residual low-rank structure
verbatim: we attach residual low-rank paths to it to compensate for compression errors and enhance task-specific accuracy ... the weights of the compressed model are not modified during compensation.
paraphrase: Residual structure: W_eff = W_hat + A B^T; compressed weights are frozen; only A,B added.
preconditions: Compressed backbone shared across tasks; lightweight low-rank modules attached per task.
consequences: Allows multi-adapter inference (e.g., vLLM); compressed weights must remain bit-exact.

label: eigenspace projection
verbatim: we propose projecting the compression error into the eigenspace of the corresponding layer's input activations, ensuring a direct alignment between the error approximation loss (Eq. 2) and the overall layer-wise model compression loss (Eq. 1).
paraphrase: Project Delta into the eigenspace of X_tilde X_tilde^T weighted by sqrt(Lambda) before SVD; eigenvalues act as importance scores.
preconditions: X_tilde = mean of input activations over calibration set; X_tilde X_tilde^T symmetric PSD.
consequences: Spec V_corr construction must use sqrt(Lambda)-weighted projection, not raw X X^T (the latter requires positive-definiteness as in SVD-LLM).

label: X_tilde definition
verbatim: Following PCA, we perform the eigendecomposition on X_tilde X_tilde^T where X_tilde in R^{n x d} is the average of the input activations over the task-specific calibration set.
paraphrase: X_tilde is the AVERAGE of input activations across the calibration set (not concatenation, not raw X).
preconditions: Calibration data tokenized at length 2048; averaged per layer.
consequences: Implementation must average activations, not concatenate; differs from SVD-LLM/Hessian construction.

label: eigenvalues as importance scores
verbatim: we leverage the eigenvalues of each activation channel as importance scores to indicate the importance of each column after the eigenprojection. This allows us to allocate more low-rank representation capacity to approximate the more critical error elements.
paraphrase: Diagonal sqrt(Lambda) weighting allocates SVD truncation capacity to high-eigenvalue (high-variance) directions.
preconditions: Lambda from eigendecomposition of X_tilde X_tilde^T.
consequences: Truncation error in projected space is weighted by eigenvalues; ensures small-eigenvalue directions are de-emphasized.

label: Q' invertible / consolidation
verbatim: Q' is invertible because Q'^{-1} = sqrt(Lambda)^{-1} U^T, and Q' Q'^{-1} = sqrt(Lambda) sqrt(Lambda)^{-1} = I, and since U is an orthogonal matrix, U U^T also yields the identity matrix. The product of B' and Q'^{-1} can be consolidated into a single matrix with the same dimensions as the original B', ensuring no additional inference latency.
paraphrase: Inverse projection sqrt(Lambda)^{-1} U^T applied to B' yields final B with same shape; no extra runtime cost.
consequences: If any Lambda_i ~ 0, sqrt(Lambda)^{-1} blows up; spec needs numerical floor (e.g., damping).

label: per-linear-layer application
verbatim: EoRA compensation is applied to each compressed linear layer, and the overall fine-tuning-free optimization of Eq. 3 across all linear layers can be completed in just a few minutes.
paraphrase: EoRA runs independently on every compressed linear layer (q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate, up, down).
consequences: Spec Stage 4 must iterate over all linear sub-modules including MoE expert projections.

label: EoRA quantization-robust
verbatim: the low-rank matrices of EoRA are robust to quantization, which can further reduce the additional memory/inference cost.
paraphrase: Quantizing the A,B factors to 4-bit causes negligible accuracy drop.
preconditions: EoRA factors stored separately from compressed backbone.
consequences: A,B can be int4-packed in production deployments.

label: EoRA as LoRA init
verbatim: with additional computational resources, users can leverage the low-rank matrices from EoRA as initialization for LoRA fine-tuning, enabling further accuracy improvements for compressed models.
paraphrase: EoRA's A,B can seed LoRA fine-tuning, outperforming QLoRA and LoftQ initializations.
preconditions: Compressed model frozen during fine-tuning; only A,B tuned.
consequences: Optional Stage-after-4 fine-tuning path.

label: calibration size WikiText2
verbatim: We sample 128 concatenated sentences of length 2048 from the WikiText2 training set as the calibration set for EoRA, Act-S, and ApiQ for the language generation task.
paraphrase: WikiText2 calibration: 128 sequences of length 2048.
consequences: Default calibration size for perplexity tasks.

label: calibration size ARC-C
verbatim: we sample 32 concatenated sentences of length 2048 from the ARC training set and combine them with 32 concatenated sentences of the same length from C4 to construct the calibration set for EoRA, Act-S, and ApiQ.
paraphrase: ARC-C calibration: 32 ARC-train + 32 C4 sequences, each length 2048 (total 64).
consequences: Mixed task+general-domain calibration.

label: calibration size math reasoning
verbatim: we sample 32 concatenated sentences of length 2048 from the MathQA/GSM8K training set and combine them with 32 concatenated sentences from C4 to form the calibration set.
paraphrase: Math calibration: 32 task-train + 32 C4 sequences, each length 2048 (total 64).
consequences: Default 64-sample calibration for math tasks.

label: default rank
verbatim: Rank of all the compensation methods is set to 128
paraphrase: Default EoRA rank r = 128 across all main results.
consequences: Spec Stage 4 default rank = 128.

label: rank ablation grid
verbatim: We vary the rank of EoRA in {64,128,256,512}
paraphrase: Rank ablation grid {64, 128, 256, 512}.
consequences: Acceptable rank values for spec; gains scale with rank.

label: calibration size ablation
verbatim: We vary the calibration size in {16,32,64} (text); Table 8 expands to {16,32,64,128,256,512}.
paraphrase: Calibration size ablation grid {16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512}.
consequences: Saturation around 128-256; 64 sufficient for most cases.

label: hardware
verbatim: All experiments are conducted on a single NVIDIA H100 GPU.
paraphrase: Single H100 GPU for all reported experiments.
consequences: Spec Stage 4 must fit on single H100/H200; no multi-GPU.

label: compression methods
verbatim: compressed using SparseGPT [6], a widely adopted pruning method, and GPTQ [1] for quantization. Channel-wise asymmetric quantization is applied across all experiments.
paraphrase: Pruning via SparseGPT (2:4); quantization via GPTQ with channel-wise asymmetric scheme; Wanda also tested.
consequences: Spec Stage 4 must accept GPTQ-compressed weights; channel-wise asymmetric scaling assumed.

label: baselines
verbatim: We compare EoRA with ZeroQuant-V2 [10] which proposes using simple SVD for optimizing Eq. 2 ... we refer to [scaled-error variant of ASVD] as Act-S ... a training-based method, ApiQ [21].
paraphrase: Baselines: ZeroQuant-V2 (plain SVD on Delta), Act-S (ASVD-derived activation scaling), ApiQ (gradient-trained low-rank).
consequences: Spec must benchmark against these three; Act-S derivation given in §4.1.

label: LoRA fine-tuning settings
verbatim: The rank of LoRA is set to 128 and is applied to every linear layer, initialized using EoRA, SVD following LoftQ [9], and standard initialization following QLoRA [11]. ... We fine-tune the models for 3 epochs with a batch size of 64, a learning rate of 1e-5, and a cosine learning rate scheduler.
paraphrase: Optional LoRA fine-tuning: rank 128, all linear layers, 3 epochs, batch 64, lr 1e-5, cosine schedule.
consequences: Recipe for downstream LoRA refinement post-EoRA.

label: evaluation framework
verbatim: We evaluate EoRA and the baselines ... using the LM-Evaluation-Harness framework [22].
paraphrase: Evaluation: lm-evaluation-harness on WikiText2 (PPL), ARC-C, MathQA, GSM8K.
consequences: Spec Stage 4 evaluation must use lm-evaluation-harness for parity.

label: Act-S baseline scaling
verbatim: we scale the compression error Delta using a diagonal scaling matrix S, where each diagonal entry s_i is computed based on the average absolute value of the activations X_tilde in the i-th channel as s_i = ( (1/n) sum_{j=1}^n |X_tilde_{ij}| )^{1/2}. We then apply SVD to the scaled error Delta'' = Delta S to obtain its low-rank approximation. Since S is invertible, we can project the approximation back to the original space as Delta = Delta'' S^{-1} ~ A'' B''^T S^{-1}.
paraphrase: Act-S = ASVD-derived baseline: scale Delta by sqrt(mean |X_tilde|) per channel, SVD, then unscale.
consequences: Reference baseline; differs from EoRA by using L1 channel scale vs. eigenvalue weighting.

label: Table 1 LLaMA3-8B 2:4
paraphrase: Sparsity compensation main results, rank 128.
consequences: EoRA dominates all fine-tuning-free baselines.

label: Table 2 LLaMA3-8B W4
paraphrase: GPTQ W4 quantization compensation, rank 128.
consequences: EoRA best on MathQA and PPL; competitive on ARC-C/GSM8K.

label: Table 2 LLaMA3-8B W3
paraphrase: GPTQ W3 quantization compensation, rank 128.
consequences: EoRA recovers 10.84% ARC-C, 6.74% MathQA, 11.45% GSM8K (relative to uncompensated).

label: Table 3 LoRA-init
verbatim: LLaMA3-8B GPTQ W4 fine-tuning: Standard 56.39/53.56; QLoRA 54.09/51.42; LoftQ 54.52/53.96; EoRA-init 55.46/56.04 on ARC-C/MathQA.
paraphrase: EoRA initialization beats QLoRA and LoftQ; matches full-precision fine-tuning on MathQA.
consequences: Justifies EoRA as superior LoRA seed.

label: Table 4 LLaMA2-7B 2:4
paraphrase: Sparsity comp on LLaMA2-7B 2:4.
consequences: Generalizes across model sizes.

label: Table 4 LLaMA2-13B 2:4
paraphrase: Sparsity comp on LLaMA2-13B 2:4.
consequences: EoRA best on PPL/ARC-C/MathQA.

label: Table 6 LLaMA3-8B 2:4 + W4
paraphrase: Combined SparseGPT 2:4 + GPTQ W4 compensation.
preconditions: Severe compression; baseline collapses to random.
consequences: EoRA reduces PPL from 86.15 to 12.60; +12.88/+9.69/+10.16 on tasks.

label: Table 7 Wanda 2:4
paraphrase: Compatibility with Wanda pruning.
consequences: EoRA compatible with multiple pruning methods.

label: Table 8 calib ablation W4
paraphrase: Calibration size effect on MathQA, W4. Saturates ~256.
consequences: Diminishing returns past 128.

label: Table 8 calib ablation W3
paraphrase: Calibration size effect on MathQA, W3.
consequences: Saturates ~128.

label: Table 8 calibration time
paraphrase: EoRA wall-clock per #calib samples.
consequences: Even rank-128 compensation finishes in <15 minutes.

label: Table 9 kernel 3-bit
verbatim: LLaMA3-70B 3-bit per-token latency: FP16=60ms; 3-bit only=35ms (1.7x); EoRA r64 noKernel=52ms (1.2x), Kernel=44ms (1.4x); r128 noKernel=54ms (1.1x), Kernel=43ms (1.4x); r256 noKernel=58ms (1x), Kernel=48ms (1.3x).
paraphrase: Custom CUDA kernel restores 1.4x speedup at rank 128 / 3-bit.
consequences: Kernel essential to retain quantization speedup.

label: Table 9 kernel 4-bit
verbatim: 4-bit per-token latency: 4-bit only=38ms (1.6x); EoRA r64 noKernel=60ms/Kernel=49ms (1.2x); r128 noKernel=61ms/Kernel=51ms (1.2x); r256 noKernel=63ms/Kernel=53ms (1.1x).
paraphrase: Without kernel, EoRA can be slower than FP16; kernel restores ~1.2x.
consequences: Memory-bound issue confirmed.

label: Table 10 rank ablation LLaMA3-8B 2:4
paraphrase: EoRA monotonically improves with rank up to 512.
consequences: Rank 512 closes most of the gap to FP16 (50.42/40.10/36.23).

label: 1.4x kernel speedup
verbatim: We also introduce an optimized CUDA kernel, accelerating inference by up to 1.4x and reducing memory overhead through quantizing EoRA.
paraphrase: Custom kernel + EoRA quantization yield up to 1.4x inference speedup over FP16.
consequences: Optional optimization; not required for correctness.

label: EoRA quantized 4-bit robust
verbatim: when a 512-rank EoRA is quantized from 16-bits to 4-bit on 2:4 pruned LLaMA3-8B, the accuracy drops are only 0.43% on ARC-C while the total model size reduces by 16.49%.
paraphrase: Quantizing EoRA factors to 4-bit costs <0.5% ARC-C while saving 16% model size.
consequences: Recommendation: quantize EoRA to 4-bit by default.

label: no extra latency in theory
verbatim: The product of B' and Q'^{-1} can be consolidated into a single matrix with the same dimensions as the original B', ensuring no additional inference latency
paraphrase: After compensation, B = B' Q'^{-1} has same shape as B'; no runtime extra ops vs. plain LoRA.
consequences: Inference cost identical to LoRA-style residuals.

label: vs SVD-LLM
verbatim: SVD-LLM [15] is conceptually close to our work ... It relies on the matrix product of the activation being positive-definite, a condition often unmet in practice ... In contrast, EoRA employs eigendecomposition, which only requires the matrix product of the activation to be symmetric—a property that naturally holds.
paraphrase: EoRA's eigendecomposition-based approach avoids SVD-LLM's positive-definiteness assumption.
consequences: More numerically robust than SVD-LLM.

label: EoRA approximates compression error not weights
verbatim: Whereas prior methods aim to replace pre-trained weight matrices with low-rank approximations to reduce model size and inference cost, EoRA focuses on approximating the compression error itself.
paraphrase: Goal of EoRA = approximate Delta = W - W_hat (residual), NOT W directly.
consequences: Distinguishes EoRA from FWSVD/ASVD/SVD-LLM as compression methods.

label: minutes-scale optimization
verbatim: the optimization time for both EoRA and Act-S is comparable, with each completing within minutes, whereas ApiQ requires over hours to optimize ... significantly faster optimization time (15 minutes vs. 2.5 hours).
paraphrase: EoRA finishes in ~15 minutes per LLaMA3-8B; ApiQ takes ~2.5 hours.
consequences: Spec Stage 4 budget should be on the order of minutes, not hours.

label: paper notation for V_corr
verbatim: Output: A', B: Two low-rank matrices for compensation. ... A' = U' Sigma'  ;  B = V'^T Q'^{-1}  ;  forward: X W_hat^T + X B^T A'^T
paraphrase: Paper uses A' (left, rank x out) and B (right, in x rank, after back-projection); the spec's 'V_corr' corresponds to the right factor B (the residual correction projected back via Q'^{-1}).
consequences: Spec must align V_corr := B = V'^T Q'^{-1} (or equivalently V'^T sqrt(Lambda)^{-1} U^T) as the right-hand low-rank factor.

label: compressed weights frozen
verbatim: the weights of the compressed model are not modified during compensation. This enables deployment of a single, general compressed backbone alongside lightweight, task-specific low-rank modules.
paraphrase: W_hat is bit-exact frozen; only A,B added.
consequences: Spec must verify W_hat hash before/after Stage 4 is unchanged.

label: 3-bit accuracy recovery numbers
verbatim: achieving notable accuracy improvements (e.g., 10.84% on ARC-Challenge, 6.74% on MathQA, and 11.45% on GSM8K for LLaMA3-8B compressed to 3-bit).
paraphrase: EoRA at rank 128 recovers +10.84/+6.74/+11.45 absolute accuracy on ARC-C/MathQA/GSM8K for LLaMA3-8B GPTQ-W3.
consequences: Stage 4 quality gate: must approach these gains within tolerance.

label: 2:4 sparsity recovery
verbatim: EoRA with rank 128 boosts the accuracy of the LLaMA3-8B model pruned to 2:4 structured sparsity by 4.53%, 3.48%, and 11.83% on ARC-C, MathQA, and GSM8K, respectively—all achieved within minutes using just 64 calibration samples per task.
paraphrase: Rank-128 EoRA recovers +4.53/+3.48/+11.83 on ARC-C/MathQA/GSM8K for SparseGPT 2:4 LLaMA3-8B with 64 calibration samples.
consequences: Quality target for Stage 4 sparsity compensation.

label: PCA-inspired importance
verbatim: Inspired by the classical Principal Component Analysis (PCA) algorithm, we leverage the eigenvalues of each activation channel as importance scores
paraphrase: Eigenvalues of X_tilde X_tilde^T act as PCA-style importance weights over channels.
consequences: Justifies sqrt(Lambda) weighting in Q'.

label: Eckart-Young optimality
verbatim: By the Eckart-Young theorem [16], the minimization of this Frobenius norm is achieved by running SVD on Delta', therefore, we prove that minimizing ||Delta' - A' B'^T|| via SVD is equivalent to minimizing Eq. 6.
paraphrase: Eckart-Young theorem provides global optimality of SVD truncation for rank-constrained Frobenius approximation in the eigenspace.
consequences: EoRA is provably optimal for Eq.6 within rank-r class.

label: per-layer notation
verbatim: For simplicity, we omit the subscript l, which corresponds to layer l in the following sections.
paraphrase: All formulas are per-layer; layer subscript suppressed throughout §3.
consequences: Implementation must repeat per linear sub-module.
